description: Adapt both notebooks to the new dataset (final_bengali_comments_vulgar_threat_insult_neutral.csv) and fix hyperparameters to resolve training instability and under-fitting in Track 1, and reduce over-regularization in Track 2.
steps: TRACK 1 (bengali-cyberbullying-lightweight-v4.ipynb) - DATASET ADAPTATION:

1. Cell 0 (markdown): Rewrite the intro. Remove all mentions of troll+insult merge / flaming. Update to describe 3 toxic labels (vulgar, threat, insult) with neutral derived. Remove the MERGE_TROLL_INSULT references. Update the honest ceiling estimate. Mention the new dataset (12.7k rows, 4 classes).

2. Cell 2 (Config class): Change DATA_PATH to 'final_bengali_comments_vulgar_threat_insult_neutral.csv'. Change LABEL_COLS to ['vulgar', 'threat', 'insult', 'neutral']. Change TOXIC_COLS to ['vulgar', 'threat', 'insult']. Change NUM_OUT to 3. REMOVE MERGE_TROLL_INSULT entirely. Update hyperparameters (see below).

3. Cell 3 (data loading): Remove all MERGE_TROLL_INSULT logic (the if block that creates 'flaming'). Add encoding='utf-8-sig' to pd.read_csv OR handle the BOM in the column name (rename column \ufefftext to text). Add data cleaning: drop rows where all label columns are 0 (2 rows). For the 1 row with both toxic and neutral=1, set neutral=0 (since neutral is derived anyway). Since neutral is derived as NOT(any toxic), we do NOT need the neutral column from CSV - we just need vulgar, threat, insult. After reading CSV, rename BOM column if needed, then verify TOXIC_COLS exist.
4. Cell 5 (Split): Update LABEL_COLS references. Since MERGE_TROLL_INSULT is gone, LABEL_COLS is already set correctly from Config.
5. Cell 7 (Augmentation): Remove references to MERGE_TROLL_INSULT.
6. Cell 14 (Training loop): The training loop references cfg.TOXIC_COLS so it should work with the new 3-class setup without changes beyond what Config provides.
7. Cells 16-17 (Threshold + Test evaluation): The classification report mentions 5 classes - update to 4 classes (vulgar, threat, insult, neutral). Update any hardcoded class name lists.
8. Cell 19 (Ensemble): Update class name references if any.
9. Cell 20 (Save): Update summary text if it mentions flaming/troll.
TRACK 1 - HYPERPARAMETER FIXES (all in Cell 2 Config class):
10. REMOVE RDROP_ALPHA entirely (set to 0.0) - R-Drop doubles compute and over-constrains a model that cannot learn.
11. REMOVE MIXUP_ALPHA (set to 0.0) - Mixup blurs labels for already-difficult minority classes.
12. Increase UNFREEZE_BODY_LR_FACTOR: 0.15 -> 0.30
13. Increase UNFREEZE_EMB_LR_FACTOR: 0.02 -> 0.05
14. Increase base LR: 5e-4 -> 8e-4
15. Reduce WEIGHT_DECAY: 3e-3 -> 1e-3
16. Reduce DROPOUT: 0.55 -> 0.45
17. Reduce LABEL_SMOOTHING: 0.10 -> 0.05
18. Increase EPOCHS: 25 -> 35
19. Increase PATIENCE: 5 -> 8
20. Keep GRAD_CLIP at 0.5 (or can increase to 1.0).

21. In Cell 13 (lr_lambda function): Replace cosine annealing with warm restarts with SIMPLE cosine decay (no restarts). Remove the cycle_steps logic. Just use a single cosine from warmup_end to total_steps.
22. DROPOUT_EMB: keep at 0.45 or reduce slightly to 0.35.
TRACK 2 (bengali-cyberbullying-transformer.ipynb) - DATASET ADAPTATION:
23. Cell 0 (markdown): Same as Track 1 - remove troll/flaming references, update class description.

24. Cell 2 (Config class): Change DATA_PATH. Change LABEL_COLS to ['vulgar', 'threat', 'insult', 'neutral']. Change TOXIC_COLS to ['vulgar', 'threat', 'insult']. Change NUM_OUT to 3. REMOVE MERGE_TROLL_INSULT.
25. Cell 3 (data loading): Remove MERGE_TROLL_INSULT logic. Handle BOM (use encoding='utf-8-sig' or rename column). Drop 2 all-zero rows. Derive neutral correctly.
26. Update any print statements / comments that mention troll or flaming.
TRACK 2 - HYPERPARAMETER FIXES (Cell 2 Config):
27. Reduce RDROP_ALPHA: 1.0 -> 0.3
28. Reduce LABEL_SMOOTHING: 0.10 -> 0.05
29. Reduce FREEZE_LAYERS: 4 -> 2 (let more layers train)
30. Increase PATIENCE: 3 -> 5
31. Increase EPOCHS: 8 -> 12 (more room to converge)
32. Keep LR at 1.5e-5 (transformer LR is already reasonable).
33. Reduce WEIGHT_DECAY: 0.03 -> 0.01
BOTH NOTEBOOKS - VALIDATION:
34. Clear all cell outputs (set outputs to [] for code cells) to keep the notebook clean since data changed.
35. Verify both notebooks are valid JSON.
36. Verify all code cells parse as valid Python (ast.parse).
acceptance_criteria: No references to MERGE_TROLL_INSULT remain in either notebook
No references to 'troll' as a label column remain (mentions in comments explaining history are OK)
No references to 'flaming' as a label remain
Track 2 Config: same label changes as Track 1
Track 1 Config: RDROP_ALPHA = 0.0 (or RDROP_ALPHA removed), MIXUP_ALPHA = 0.0 (or removed)
Track 1 Config: LR = 8e-4, WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-3, DROPOUT = 0.45, LABEL_SMOOTHING = 0.05
Track 1: lr_lambda uses simple cosine decay without warm restarts
Track 2 Config: RDROP_ALPHA = 0.3, LABEL_SMOOTHING = 0.05, FREEZE_LAYERS = 2, PATIENCE = 5, EPOCHS = 12, WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.01
BOM handling is present in data loading (either utf-8-sig encoding or column rename)
Both notebooks are valid JSON (json.load succeeds)
All code cells in both notebooks parse as valid Python (ast.parse succeeds)
Cell outputs are cleared in both notebooks
verification: python3 -c "import json; nb = json.load(open('bengali-cyberbullying-lightweight-v4.ipynb')); src = ''.join(nb['cells'][2]['source']); assert 'MERGE_TROLL_INSULT' not in src, 'MERGE_TROLL_INSULT still in Track 1 config'; assert 'RDROP_ALPHA' not in src or '0.0' in src or '= 0' in src, 'RDROP still active in Track 1'; print('Track 1 config checks pass')"

python3 -c "import json; nb = json.load(open('bengali-cyberbullying-transformer.ipynb')); src = ''.join(nb['cells'][2]['source']); assert 'MERGE_TROLL_INSULT' not in src, 'MERGE_TROLL_INSULT still in Track 2'; print('Track 2 config checks pass')"
